title: rubus heterocladus
intro: Rubus heterocladus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan bramble, is a species of shrub in the rose family that is native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce fever. The health benefits of Rubus heterocladus include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Rubus heterocladus can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
